title: Insurance Backtest: ETH-USDC Liquidity Provider Hedging
abstract: We evaluate the effectiveness of a fee-concentration insurance mechanism for passive Uniswap V4 liquidity providers. Using 180 days of mainnet ETH-USDC swap events, we simulate the Fee Concentration Index (Δ⁺) under epoch-based accumulation and compute lookback payoffs for LONG token holders. Our findings show that hedged LPs outperform unhedged LPs in 67% of epochs when Δ⁺ exceeds the strike threshold, with a mean hedge value of $0.034 per dollar deposited.

methodology.dataSource: Uniswap V3 ETH-USDC 30bps pool, 180 days of swap and position events (mainnet)
methodology.mechanism: Epoch-based Δ⁺ accumulation with destruction-by-abandonment reset. Each epoch: Θ = Σ(1/ℓ_k) over position removals, A_T = √(Θ), Δ⁺ = max(0, A_T − atNull).
methodology.payoff: Power-squared lookback: p(HWM, K) = max(0, ((HWM − K)/(1 − K))²), where HWM is the high-water mark of Δ⁺ within the epoch and K is the strike.

parameters.name: γ (gamma)
parameters.description: Payoff power exponent. Higher values concentrate payout at extreme Δ⁺ values.

parameters.name: α (alpha)
parameters.description: Payoff scaling factor. Normalizes payout to [0, 1] range.

parameters.name: epochLength
parameters.description: Epoch duration in seconds (1 day). Δ⁺ accumulators reset each epoch.

parameters.name: K (strike)
parameters.description: Strike threshold. LONG pays out only when HWM > K.

parameters.name: atNull₀
parameters.description: Estimated competitive null for ETH-USDC 30bps pool.

conclusion: Fee concentration insurance is most valuable for pools with moderate-to-high JIT activity (Δ⁺ > 0.20) and epoch lengths of 1–7 days. The power-squared lookback payoff provides convex exposure that rewards hedgers proportionally to extraction severity. At K = 0.25, 67% of epochs produce a positive hedge value, with diminishing returns below K = 0.15 due to premium costs exceeding payouts in low-concentration epochs.

_meta.description: Research section sample data. Values are realistic approximations from ETH-USDC backtest.
